Block.Para: Second Session, Fortieth Parliament,
57 Elizabeth II, 2009
SENATE OF CANADA
BILL S-203
An Act to amend the Business Development Bank of Canada Act (municipal infrastructure bonds) and to make consequential amendments to another Act
first reading, January 27, 2009
THE HONOURABLE SENATOR GRAFSTEIN

Available on the Parliament of Canada Web Site at the following address:
http://www.parl.gc.ca
SUMMARY

This enactment amends the Business Development Bank of Canada Act to provide for income tax exemptions for municipal bonds to finance infrastructure projects. The short title of the Act is also changed.

Bill_Part.RunningHead_E: Business Development Bank of Canada (municipal infrastructure bonds)

Block.Para: 2nd Session, 40th Parliament,
57 Elizabeth II, 2009
senate of canada
BILL S-203
An Act to amend the Business Development Bank of Canada Act (municipal infrastructure bonds) and to make consequential amendments to another Act
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:
BUSINESS DEVELOPMENT BANK OF CANADA ACT
1. Section 1 of the Business Development Bank of Canada Act is replaced by the following:

ExplNote.Para: Business Development Bank of Canada Act
1. This Act may be cited as the Business Development Bank of Canada Act.

Para.change: 1. This Act may be cited as the Municipal Modernization and Business Development Bank of Canada Act.

Block.Para: 2. The Act is amended by adding the following after section 4:

Block.MarginalNote: Additional purpose â municipal infrastructure bonds

Para.change: 4.1 An additional purpose of the Bank is to deal with applications, under section 33.1, for income tax exemptions for municipal bonds to finance infrastructure projects.

Block.Para: 3. The Act is amended by adding the following after section 33:

Para.change: MUNICIPAL INFRASTRUCTURE BONDS â INCOME TAX EXEMPTIONS
33.1 (1) A municipality in Canada may apply to the Bank for an income tax exemption for bonds to be issued by the municipality to finance an infrastructure project.
(2) A municipality may apply for an income tax exemption only if the municipality has received provincial approval to make the application.

(3) For the purposes of subsection (2), âprovincial approvalâ means approval by the person or body specified, for that purpose, under the legislation of the province or, if no such person or body is specified, by the government of the province.
(4) The Bank shall grant an income tax exemption if it is satisfied that
(a) the proceeds of the issue of the bonds will be used for an infrastructure project; and
(b) any other requirements prescribed by the regulations are satisfied.
(5) The Bank shall publish a notice of any income tax exemption granted under this section in the Canada Gazette.
(6) Interest paid on a bond in respect of which an income tax exemption has been granted is exempt from income tax under the Income Tax Act.
33.2 The Governor in Council may make regulations
(a) prescribing what qualifies as an infrastructure project for the purposes of section 33.1; and
(b) prescribing requirements for the purposes of paragraph 33.1(4)(b).

Block.Para: ACCESS TO INFORMATION ACT
4. Schedule II to the Access to Information Act is amended by striking out the reference to:
Business Development Bank of Canada Act

Para.I: Loi sur la Banque de dÃ©veloppement du Canada

Para.B: and the corresponding reference to âsection 37â.

Block.Para: 5. Schedule II to the Act is amended by adding, in alphabetical order, a reference to:
Municipal Modernization and Business Development Bank of Canada Act

Para.I: Loi sur la Banque de dÃ©veloppement du Canada et la modernisation des municipalitÃ©s

Para.B: and a corresponding reference in respect of that Act to âsection 37â.

Block.Para: COMING INTO FORCE
6. The provisions of this Act come into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
